the destruction of unsold goods → Waste prevention and reduction → Mandatory criteria for green public procurement → Digital Product Passport and new labeling rules → Stronger market surveillance ESPR Establishes a Framework Legislation 27.06.202511 Framework legislation Regular updates of multiannual working plans setting out priorities Product-specific measures based on detailed impact assessment ESPR is a framework of legislation It does not set specific measures. Instead, it enables their later adoption. | Source: https://environment.ec.europa.eu/events/information-session-new-ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation-espr-2024-05-22_en Empowerment to Adopt very mobile properties | Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=OJ:L_202401781#art_2 What Shall Not Be Covered by the Ecodesign for Sustainable Product Regulation (ESPR)? Article 1, Paragraph 2 This (Ecodesign for Sustainable Product) Regulation applies to any physical goods that are placed on the market or put into service, including components and intermediate products. However, it does not apply to: 27.06.202516 Food Regulation (EC) 178/2002 Feed Regulation (EC) 178/2002 Medicinal products for human use Directive 2001/83/EC Veterinary medicinal products Regulation (EU) 2019/6 Living plants, animals and micro-organisms Products of human origin Products of plants and animals relating directly to their future reproduction Vehicles Regulation (EU) 167/2012, Regulation (EU) 168/2023, Regulation (EU) 2018/858 | Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=OJ:L_202401781#art_1 What Shall Be Covered by the Ecodesign for Sustainable Product Regulation (ESPR)? Article 1, Paragraph 2 This (Ecodesign for Sustainable Product) Regulation applies to any physical goods that are placed on the market or put into service, including components and intermediate products. 27.06.202517 End-Use Products Intermediate Products ICTConstructionTextile Furniture Tires Ceramics DetergentsMattresses Lubricants Cosmetics Toys Fishing Nets & Gear Absorbent Hygiene ProductsPaints Non-Ferrous Metals ChemicalsAluminumSteel Plastic and Polymers Glass Paper Pulp and Boards Energy Related Products | Sources : https://publications.jrc.ec.europa.eu/repository/handle/JRC138903 What Shall Be Covered by the Ecodesign for Sustainable Product Regulation (ESPR)? Article 18, Paragraph 5 The Commission prioritizes the following product groups in the first working plan, adopted April 16, 2025. 27.06.202518 End-Use Products Intermediate Products ICTConstructionTextile Furniture TiresMattresses Toys AluminumSteel Energy Related Products | Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=OJ:L_202401781#art_18 Detergents What Shall be Covered by the First Working Plan 2025 – 2030? 27.06.202519 Product / Measure JRC Ranking Stakeholders’ Opinion Market Size (EU) Timeline End-Use Products Textiles / Apparel 1st High support 78 bn € (142 bn € (2019)) 2027 Furniture 2nd Support 140 bn € (2021) 2028 Tires 3rd High Support 45 bn € (2021) 2027 Mattresses 4th High Support 10 bn € (2022) 2029 Intermediate Products Iron & Steel 1st High Support 152 bn € (2023) 2026 Aluminum 4th Support 40 bn € (2019) 2027 Horizontal Requirements Reparability (incl. scoring) N/A High Support N/A 2027 Recycled content and recyclability of electrical and electronic equipment N/A Support N/A 2029 | Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:52025DC0187 The first working plan lasts 5 years (2025-2030). Next revision after 3 years in 2028 Why is a Digital Product Passport (DPP) Required Enhance transparency throughout the product lifecycle. Offers access to relevant data (materials, sustainability, carbon footprint, etc.) for all actors: manufacturers, consumers, recyclers, and authorities. It supports sustainable purchasing decisions by enabling consumers to compare products and choose the most sustainable ones based on durability or energy efficiency criteria. Ensure regulatory compliance. Facilitates monitoring and enforcement of compliance with ESPR standards and other environmental regulations. Promote the circular economy. Provide key information on products' composition and design to facilitate repair, reuse, recycling, and remanufacturing. Advance EU climate and environmental goals. It is a key tool to reduce the environmental impact of products and support the European Green Deal goals. 27.06.202520 What is the Digital Product Passport (DPP)? (see ESPR §9 - §15) 27.06.202521 DPP Registry | Source: https://youtu.be/j7nKDd8QUpA?si=vtfyYCaw9qkKyYVQ&t=721 Central Services operated by the EU Commission ICSMS CERTEX Market surveillance Systems Customs Systems Match customs declaration before Import goods Validate e.g. Declaration of Conformity Economic Operator Register Product Consumer DPP Web Portal Search DPP Decentralized Storage DPP Content, operated by the DPP System Providers Decentralized search Recycler Market Authority Data Carrier Fetch DPP Fetch DPP Search DPPs Decentralized Storage DPP Content, operated by the Economic Operator Decentralized search Resolves DPP Storage Resolves DPP Storage What is the Digital Product Passport (DPP)? E-Mail LabelSafety Datasheet+ + = Information Disruption maximal disruption More Regulations are Announced… 27.06.202540 We get hit by a regulatory tsunami Digital Material Passport (DMP) – A Gamechanger for Product Information Communication ◼ Horizontal material representation – serves multiple segments ◼ Harmonized, interoperable* and machine-readable ◼ Public and private compartment – for different data types ◼ Visibility “one level up” / “one level down” in the value chain ◼ Decentralized DMP-Data storage – protect IPR and data sovereignty ◼ Technology agnostic – Implementation with various technology stacks ◼ Enabling data provision to regulated DPPs in ESPR, CPR, and other Green Deal regulations 27.06.202541 For Chemical Product | Interoperability knows five facets: 1) Semantic (“what”), Transport (”integrate with existing systems”), expected behavior, policy, syntactic (“how”), See ISO 19941:2017 for further details. https://standards.iso.org/ittf/PubliclyAvailableStandards/c066639_ISO_IEC_19941_2017.zip Digital Material Passports Connect the Dots in the Supply Chain 27.06.202542 Digital Material Passports Connect the Dots in the Supply Chain 27.06.202543 For Chemical Product Digital Material Passports Transform Supply Chain Communication 27.06.202544 Communication via Source Refine Manufacture Assemble Use Dismantle DisposeManufacture helps to maximize the material value during the use phase and … DMPs Foster Circular Economy & Sustainability Communication S o u rce Refine ManufactureAssembleUse,R e -use,RepairCollect&Dism a ntleRecycle(R-Strategies) R&D Isn’t the DPP Just Creating Additional Costs? Yes – Material Master Data Management (MDM) Requires More Attention ◼ More interaction with upstream supply chains is required to gather raw material information ◼ Increasing IT system complexity because of the DPP System Yes, … – It Opens up Opportunities to Leverage Efficiency & Business Ideas ◼ Tighter and more frequent interactions with suppliers ◼ Transformation from document-centric to information-centric IT systems 27.06.202546 Isn’t the DPP Just Making the Process More Complicated? Yes – Different Stakeholders to be Involved during Data Exchange ◼ Additional information requirements on sustainability and circularity metrics ◼ Additional information requirements during the product lifecycle ◼ More complicated and frequently changing product labels Yes, … – But You Can Tighten Your Relationship With Supply Chain Actors ◼ But you can tighten your relationship with supply chain actors ◼ More touch points with customers and users during the product lifecycle ◼ Direct channel to communicated value proposition by DPP to customer & generate brand awareness 27.06.202547 The DPP System Integrates in Your Existing Architecture 27.06.202548 Data Staging – consolidate data from different sources Hazardous Substances Environmental information Product Information Manuals, Documents Product Identity Operations Events Manufacturer’s DBs (ERP, PIM, etc.) Manufacturer’s DBs (ERP, PIM, etc.) Manufacturer’s DBs (ERP, PIM, etc.) Supplier DB Supplier DB Content Management Access and Role Management DPP framework Stores / WarehousesWeb Pages / App Online Sales Channels Physical Sales / Distribution Customer / User DPP System DPP DPP Backup System | Source: Adoption from Dominik Halbeisen @ UNECE PWI 25534-1, see https://unece.org/sites/default/files/2025-03/Consolidated%20Slidedeck%20of%20the%20AdHoc%20Meeting%20on%20DPP.pdf End PointsSourcesProduct Information Sharing Implement existing Standards to Enable Digital Material Passports to Become a Game Changer for the industry! 27.06.202549 Make more (…) of the materials and products we generate ◼ Use ◼ Benefits ◼ Greenhouse Gas Reduction Check list 1. Is there a viable Business Case? 2. Are you an Economic Operator? 3. Are your products falling under the ESPR? 4. Are you aware of the data required to report on the Ecodesign Requirements? 5. Are you aware of the product categories' priorities and timelines? 6. Do you possess the relevant information, performance, and additional requirements? 7. Is your MDM / PIM ready to host the DPP-Data? 8. Do you know the downstream actors and their R&Rs along the product lifecycle? 9. Are you aware of the market surveillance mechanism? 10. Can you issue a unique identifier for the granularity required? 11. Can you back up DPPs? 12. Can you offer supplementary services? 13. Is there a viable Business Case? 27.06.202550 Take Home Messages ◼ One harmonized standard digital material representation ◼ DPP Information shall be sovereign and shall belong to the economic operator ◼ Structure DPP along the product lifecycle phases  Production – no DPP is available & material data is shared in harmonized form  Market Introduction – DPP is issued  End of Life – DPP is revoked, and material accounting starts ◼ DPP’s data is required for material accounting (sustainability & circularity metrics) ◼ DPPs serve R-Strategies in line with the “need to know” principle ◼ DPPs are not a track-and-trace tool 27.06.202551 Sources in the order of presentation ◼ EU Green Deal – https://youtu.be/F2Z7rio5sow?si=ZK8jhl6a6SvvM-Rx ◼ EU Green Deal Communication –
